name: use-focus-trap
title: useFocusTrap
description: Trap Tab/Shift+Tab focus within a container element. Auto-focuses first focusable element on mount, restores focus on unmount.

content.text: Options for trapping Tab focus inside a container.
Element that receives focus when the trap activates.
Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap releases.
Whether the focus trap is active.
Per-document trap stacks: only the top entry captures focus.
Splice an incoming ancestor before its existing descendant so the descendant
stays on top; otherwise activation order makes the new trap the top.
* Keeps Tab and Shift+Tab focus inside a container while active, with nested
* trap stacking and optional focus restoration on release.
Detach listeners BEFORE focus moves out in release(), or the document-level
focusin recapture re-traps the restored target. restoreOnUnmount:false keeps
this stack hook from moving focus during its own unmount cleanup.
No dependency array on purpose: React does not re-fire effects when
containerRef.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
restoreFocus is release-time policy; update in place instead of tearing
down (which would recapture the interior as the restore target, not the opener).
Capture the opener before focus moves inside, so a false-to-true toggle can still restore to it.
Push BEFORE arming/focusing: pushTrap suspends the previous top so its
focusin handler does not recapture focus away from this new trap.
Detach own listeners BEFORE removeTrap resumes the outer trap; otherwise
our focusin sees the outer trap's refocus as an escape and fights it.

docs.description: Trap Tab/Shift+Tab focus within a container element. Auto-focuses first focusable element on mount, restores focus on unmount.

parameters.name: containerRef
parameters.description: Ref to the container element that traps focus. Passed as the first positional argument.

parameters.name: options.initialFocus
parameters.description: Ref to the element that receives focus when the trap activates. Defaults to the first focusable element.

parameters.name: options.restoreFocus
parameters.description: Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap deactivates.

parameters.name: options.enabled
parameters.description: Whether the focus trap is active.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.

notes.title: Standalone
notes.content: useFocusTrap does not require KeyboardProvider. It intercepts Tab/Shift+Tab with a capture-phase keydown listener on the container's document while the trap is active, and uses a capture-phase focusin listener there to recapture escaped focus.

notes.title: Tab wrapping
notes.content: When focus reaches the last focusable element, Tab wraps to the first. Shift+Tab from the first wraps to the last.

content.text: Tab cycles through buttons; focus is trapped here.
initialFocus moves focus to Save instead of the first focusable element.
This dialog opens with focus on Save. Tab stays inside; Esc is up to you.

parameters.name: containerRef
parameters.description: Ref to the container element that traps focus. Passed as the first positional argument.

parameters.name: options.initialFocus
parameters.description: Ref to the element that receives focus when the trap activates. Defaults to the first focusable element.

parameters.name: options.restoreFocus
parameters.description: Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap deactivates.

parameters.name: options.enabled
parameters.description: Whether the focus trap is active.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.
